title: Sober Living Homes - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Sober Living Homes 21 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Men Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ARBOR HOUSE PORTLAND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a culturally and gender-specific sober living home environment. Residents have access to peer support and mental health services, resume-building and vocational guidance, assistance navigating social services and criminal and family courts, and connection to the local recovery community. Must agree to attend biweekly meetings with house leadership and at least 2 outside recovery-based mutual support meetings per week. Eligibility: Individuals who identify as female or femme who are in recovery from substance use disorder Hours: No standard hours Email (423) 598-3678 Visit Website More Details BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC LAKEVIEW VILLAGE APARTMENTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides National Alliance for Recovery Residences (NARR) accredited affordable recovery studio and two bedroom apartments combined with peer-based recovery support. 155 S Empire Boulevard Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Individuals in recovery from substance abuse use disorders and their families. Household income limits apply. Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-4pm Email (541) 756-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLANCHET HOUSE OF HOSPITALITY BLANCHET FARM RESIDENTIAL RECOVERY BLANCHET FARM PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clean and sober housing and a working farm offering support that will allow a person to gain sobriety and take the necessary steps to become self-sufficient again. AA and NA support groups help build a foundation in recovery and the connection of friendships by working and living together towards a common goal. 11750 NE Finn Hill Loop Carlton, OR 97111 Eligibility: Men age 18 and older Hours: Farm Manager: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm Case Manager: Tuesday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 241-4340 11 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE CLACKAMAS COUNTY OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing, treatment, and supportive services for individuals recovering from addiction. Programs are led by peer mentors. Intensive outpatient (IOP) and outpatient substance use disorder treatment are available at multiple locations. 707 Main Street Suite 220 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE SPECIAL PROGRAMS OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing, treatment, and supportive services for individuals recovering from addiction. Programs are led by peer mentors. Intensive outpatient (IOP) and outpatient substance use disorder treatment are available at multiple locations. 229 NE 181st Avenue Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE WASHINGTON COUNTY OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing, treatment, and supportive services for individuals recovering from addiction. Programs are led by peer mentors. Intensive outpatient (IOP) and outpatient substance use disorder treatment are available at multiple locations. 1049 SW Baseline Street Suite D480 Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN HOUSING AND DEVELOPMENT SUPPORTIVE HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of affordable housing options, including transitional housing and permanent supportive housing. These options combine affordable housing with essential services such as health care, recovery support, employment assistance, and veteran services. Also provides subsidized rental units to help ensure long-term housing stability 523 NW Everett Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness, individuals in recovery, those struggling with mental health challenges, living with HIV/AIDS or other disabling conditions, and/or a history of criminal involvement, and veterans experiencing homelessness Hours: Varies by property Email (503) 548-4931 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON ALCOHOLISM FOUNDATION SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides residential and outpatient treatment for adults with drug and/or alcohol addictions. Provides evaluations for people arrested for DUIs, Minor in Possession (MIP) information classes, two transitional living facilities (one for men, one for women and children), and alcohol- and drug-free apartments for those who have successfully completed treatment. The residential program includes a child development program for men, women, and their children. 216 SW Hailey Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who meet criteria for residential alcohol and drug addiction treatment. Mothers and single fathers referred by DHS Child Welfare may have children approved to reside with them. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 278-2558 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FREE ON THE OUTSIDE FREE ON THE OUTSIDE HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing and sober living facilities. Clients may not use illegal drugs or alcohol during their stay. 529 Molalla Avenue Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Anyone experiencing housing instability. Does not turn people away for their criminal history or credit. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 383-1834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSE OF HOPE MINISTRIES HOUSING PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers supportive housing and alcohol and drug-free shared homes. 724 SW 14th Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Age 18 and older men who are sober and drug-free Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 390-1594 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IRON TRIBE NETWORK CLEAN AND SOBER HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sober living for people in recovery. 11763 SE 82nd Drive Suite A Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with minor children Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 344-6710 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MENTAL HEALTH AND ADDICTION CERTIFICATION BOARD OF OREGON OREGON RECOVERY RESIDENCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides up to date information on recovery housing in Oregon; information is updated by agencies and recovery residence operators. 2209 Lloyd Center Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details MISSION TEENS INC NORTHWEST MBTC THE MISSION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a Christian-based transitional sober housing program up to 12 months, with structured stages supporting long-term recovery. Includes an initial 60-day blackout period with no phone or visitors to help residents focus on adjustment and recovery, and requires 5.5 hours of daily Bible study. 35045 SW Bald Peak Road Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Age 18 and older in recovery from substance abuse disorders. Must not have a registered sex offense. Hours: Phone: 7 days per week 7am-10pm Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 632-1953 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC RECOVERY VILLAGE CENTER MEDICAL DETOX MEDICAL DETOX AND CLINICALLY MANAGED DETOX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides medical detox for adults who need assistance with detoxification from one or more substances prior to entering a residential program. This is a voluntary program and the focus is management of symptoms during detoxification. The program has licensed professional oversight, and the individual meets daily with a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner who assists with Medication Assisted Treatment. 3680 Midway Street Baker City, OR 978141466 Eligibility: Single-parent households with children ages 0-6 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 523-4049 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OXFORD HOUSE OF OREGON OXFORD HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing for women and men in recovery from alcohol and drug addiction. There is no time limit for residency. Each resident is responsible for membership dues, and residents share household responsibilities. Houses are democratically run. There are no house managers or supervisors. Each house has an elected board, and weekly house meetings are conducted to discuss business, policies, etc. Website Portland, OR 97290 Eligibility: Must be or desire to be drug and alcohol free and be able to pay a share of rent and household expenses Hours: Varies by site Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOARING HEIGHTS RECOVERY HOMES FALCON HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sober housing. Clients work towards achieving self sufficiency, family reunification, and permanent housing by establishing stable work and rental histories. Eligibility: Single men and men with minor children Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 719-4963 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOARING HEIGHTS RECOVERY HOMES OSPREY HOME Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sober housing. Clients work towards achieving self sufficiency, family reunification, and permanent housing by establishing stable work and rental histories. Eligibility: Men and men with minor children Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 719-4963 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOARING HEIGHTS RECOVERY HOMES ROBIN'S NEST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sober housing. Clients work towards achieving self sufficiency, family reunification, and permanent housing by establishing stable work and rental histories. Eligibility: Single women and women with minor children Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 719-4963 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOARING HEIGHTS RECOVERY HOMES SONGBIRD HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sober housing. Clients work towards achieving self sufficiency, family reunification, and permanent housing by establishing stable work and rental histories. Eligibility: Women and women with minor children Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 719-4963 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSCENDING HOPE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a structured housing environment for those seeking recovery from addiction, alcoholism, incarceration, and/or mental health issues. 29 locations, including Aid & Assist Community Restoration, and culturally responsive Latino and Latina housing. 347A 1st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Must be working towards abstinence Hours: Not applicable Email (971) 449-6793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITIONALHOUSING.ORG TRANSITIONAL AND SOBER LIVING HOMES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers information on transitional and sober housing. Also provides an addiction counseling hotline. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (855) 860-3119 Visit Website More Details BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC LAKEVIEW VILLAGE APARTMENTS BLANCHET HOUSE OF HOSPITALITY BLANCHET FARM PROGRAM BRIDGES TO CHANGE CLACKAMAS COUNTY OFFICE BRIDGES TO CHANGE SPECIAL PROGRAMS OFFICE BRIDGES TO CHANGE WASHINGTON COUNTY OFFICE CENTRAL CITY CONCERN SUPPORTIVE HOUSING EASTERN OREGON ALCOHOLISM FOUNDATION SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES FREE ON THE OUTSIDE FREE ON THE OUTSIDE HOUSING HOUSE OF HOPE MINISTRIES HOUSING PROGRAMS IRON TRIBE NETWORK CLEAN AND SOBER HOUSING MENTAL HEALTH AND ADDICTION CERTIFICATION BOARD OF OREGON OREGON RECOVERY RESIDENCES MISSION TEENS INC THE MISSION NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC MEDICAL DETOX AND CLINICALLY MANAGED DETOX OXFORD HOUSE OF OREGON OXFORD HOUSE TRANSCENDING HOPE TRANSCENDING HOPE
